text.title: Code Review Cycle Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Code Review Cycle application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Review MUST run 3 steps (pre-screen → parallel reviewers → merge); skipping the merge is rejected.
Every finding MUST carry severity ∈ {BLOCK, WARN, NOTE} + cited line + suggested fix.
rule.rationale: Free-text findings can't be prioritised or auto-actioned.

rule.statement: Every BLOCK finding MUST be addressed by a human before merge; AI cannot self-clear BLOCKs.
rule.rationale: AI clearing its own blocks is the rubber-stamp anti-pattern.

rule.statement: Merge step MUST writeback novel patterns to patterns.md and novel mistakes to mistakes.md.
Business-logic and security-context judgement MUST be human; AI advises only.
